svg:
Directory Structure, Protection, File System Implementation: File System Structure, File System Operations, File System Internals: File Systems
Created with love
<section data-narration="
A file system consists of several components:
Inode (Index Node): Contains metadata about a file, such as its name, location, size, and permissions.
Block: A block is a contiguous block of data on a disk.
File System Tree (FST): The FST is a hierarchical representation of the directory structure. " style="background-color: #87CEEB; padding: 20px; border: 1px solid #ddd; border-radius: 10px; margin-bottom: 20px;">
File System Structure
<section data-narration="
File system operations include:
Create: Creates a new file or directory.
Read: Reads data from a file.
Write: Writes data to a file.
Delete: Deletes a file or directory.
List: Lists the files and directories in a directory. " style="background-color: #C7B8EA; padding: 20px; border: 1px solid #ddd; border-radius: 10px; margin-bottom: 20px;">
File System Operations
<section data-narration="
File systems are implemented using several techniques:
Block Allocation: A block allocation scheme is used to manage disk space and allocate blocks to files.
Directory Allocation: A directory allocation scheme is used to manage the directory structure and allocate space for new files and directories. " style="background-color: #87CEEB; padding: 20px; border: 1px solid #ddd; border-radius: 10px; margin-bottom: 20px;">
File System Internals
<section data-narration="
A file system is a collection of files, directories, and other objects that are stored and managed by the operating system.
File systems can be classified into two types:
Logical File System: A logical file system is a file system that is stored in memory and provides a virtual representation of the file system.
Physical File System: A physical file system is a file system that is stored on disk and provides a physical representation of the file system. " style="background-color: #C7B8EA; padding: 20px; border: 1px solid #ddd; border-radius: 10px; margin-bottom: 20px;">
File Systems
Created with love
